Title: Senior Product Analytics & Applied AI Consultant

Senior Product Analytics & Applied AI Consultant

We are seeking a Senior Product Analytics & Applied AI Consultant (Advanced Analytics Consultant Senior) to lead the development of advanced analytics and AI-driven insights that shape product strategy, financial performance, and member outcomes. The Senior Product Analytics & Applied AI Consultant creates statistical models to predict, classify, quantify, and/or forecast business metrics with a focus on product performance, ROI measurement, and a leading indicator development.
[...]
This role sits at the intersection of product analytics, and applied AI. Leveraging modern data platforms, APIs, and automation to deliver scalable, real-time insights that inform product strategy and financial outcomes for the business and employers.
[...]
- Pioneer next‑frontier analytics and AI (e.g., agentic/LLM‑enabled workflows and real‑time modeling) to reimagine traditional statistical approaches into adaptive decision intelligence that measurably improves product ROI, cost, quality, utilization, and member outcomes
[...]
- Prepares analytical data sets in support of modeling studies and product performance evaluation across clinical, operational, and financial dimensions.
[...]
- Build, test, and validate statistical models and predictive frameworks to assess product ROI, financial impact and utilization trends.
[...]
- Develop and implement innovative algorithms and predictive frameworks to improve cost, quality, and member outcomes.
[...]
- Partner with product and business leaders to translate data into strategic recommendations and investment decisions
[...]
- Continuously improves analytical methodologies and predictive modeling algorithms.
[...]
- Collaborates with business
[...]
patterns across cost
[...]
targeted strategies.
[...]
- Leverage AI/ML models and API-enabled tools to automate insight generation, enhance forecasting accuracy and scale analytic workflows.
[...]
Preferred Skills, Capabilities, & Experiences:
[...]
- Advanced expertise in statistical modeling and machine learning using tools such as Python, R, or SAS.
- Intermediate to Advanced knowledge of Teradata, SQL, SQL Server, or equivalent cloud-based data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks).
- Experience building analytics solutions in cloud-based data environments.
[...]
- Proven ability to design robust analytical datasets and address data quality challenges to support predictive analytics, product performance measurement, and ROI evaluation.
[...]
- Demonstrated experience in managing the design, development, and continuous improvement of complex healthcare data systems, tools, and reports strongly preferred.
[...]
- Experience applying AI/ML and APIs to automate insights and support decision-making.
[...]
Elevance Health is a health company dedicated to improving lives and communities – and making healthcare simpler. We are a Fortune 25 company with a longstanding history in the healthcare industry, looking for leaders at all levels of the organization who are passionate about making an impact on our members and the communities we serve.

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- Bachelor’s Degree (preferably in information technology,
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
[...]
Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

results.title: Data Scientist AI and LLM Applications | Curate Partners | Consulting & Talent Solutions
highlights:
# Data Scientist, AI and LLM Applications
[...]
A leading organization is seeking a mid-level Data Scientist to support a high-visibility initiative focused on applying advanced artificial intelligence to complex healthcare data. This role centers on building an AI-driven utilization management criteria analyzer that helps business stakeholders understand the clinical and financial impact of changing medication utilization strategies.
[...]
The Data Scientist will work closely with technical and non-technical partners to design, develop, and evaluate large language model solutions, agent-based AI workflows, and data science models. The position combines hands-on development with direct stakeholder engagement and requires comfort operating in a fast-moving, evolving environment.
[...]
- Perform exploratory data analysis, statistical analysis, and feature engineering on large, complex healthcare datasets.
- Develop and deploy scalable machine learning models to support forecasting, simulation, and decision intelligence use cases.
- Work with both structured and unstructured data, including clinical notes and claims data.
- Support scenario modeling and “what-if” analysis to quantify the impact of different utilization management strategies.
[...]
- Build large language model pipelines to extract metrics, values, units, and contextual information from unstructured clinical data.
- Design and refine prompt engineering strategies to improve consistency, accuracy, and reliability of LLM outputs.
- Develop explainable AI solutions that include supporting evidence and traceable reasoning.
- Evaluate model outputs for accuracy, hallucination risk, and overall performance, and drive iterative improvements.
[...]
- Design and implement agent-based AI workflows that support multi-step reasoning and decision logic.
- Implement guardrails such as confidence scoring, validation checks, and fallback mechanisms.
- Orchestrate AI components to support complex analytical workflows and simulations.
[...]
- Strong proficiency in Python and SQL.
- Experience working with large datasets.
- Hands-on experience with exploratory data analysis, statistical analysis, and machine learning model development.
- Ability to analyze data and present findings to non-technical stakeholders.
[...]
- Hands-on experience working with large language models.
- Demonstrated experience with prompt engineering and LLM evaluation frameworks.
- Experience building production-level LLM solutions.
[...]
multi-step
[...]
1. What are the core responsibilities of a Data Scientist focused on AI and LLM applications?This role develops and applies AI and large language model (LLM) solutions to solve business problems and improve user experiences. Responsibilities include building prototypes, analyzing datasets, fine-tuning models, and evaluating performance. The data scientist also collaborates with engineering and product teams to operationalize AI solutions.
[...]
2. What types of AI and LLM projects are typically handled in this role?Projects may include conversational AI, document summarization, recommendation systems, semantic search, and content generation tools. The role often involves both experimentation and production-oriented model development. The focus is on creating practical and scalable AI applications.
[...]
3. What tools and technologies are commonly used?Common tools include Python, SQL, and machine learning libraries such as PyTorch or TensorFlow. LLM frameworks, vector databases, and cloud AI platforms are also frequently used. Data visualization and experimentation tools support analysis and evaluation.
[...]
4. How are LLMs integrated into business applications?LLMs are integrated through APIs, orchestration frameworks, and custom workflows that connect AI capabilities with business systems. The data scientist helps design prompts, retrieval pipelines, and evaluation methods. Scalability and reliability are important considerations.

# Senior Data Scientist

The Senior Data Scientist at XSOLIS is responsible for developing, evaluating, and deploying scalable machine learning and AI solutions using large-scale healthcare data. The Senior Data Scientist works on moderately complex initiatives, collaborates closely with product and engineering teams, and contributes to responsible, high-quality, and production-ready AI systems.

- Develop and deploy traditional machine learning models, including bias modeling, risk mitigation, and predictive analytics

- Build scalable ML models and end-to-end ML pipelines suitable for production environments

- Perform advanced data quality assessments, validation checks, and feature identification and analysis

- Evaluate bias in training data and recommend mitigation strategies aligned with responsible AI practices

- Design and implement intermediate models for risk scoring and predictive insights

- Apply Generative AI techniques, including prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and deployment of GenAI models

- Utilize and contribute to LLM evaluation frameworks to assess performance, accuracy, and reliability

- Collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, and Business teams to identify, refine, and deliver high-impact AI use cases

### Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities)

- Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills

- Ability to collaborate cross-functionally with product-focused teams

- Clear written and verbal communication skills

- Commitment to ethical, responsible, and high-quality AI development

- Big data querying experience using major cloud providers

- Data processing, cleaning, feature extraction, and exploratory data analysis

- Prompt engineering and prompt optimization

- RAG vs. non-RAG architectures

- Experience with AI agents, agentic AI systems, and multi-agent orchestration

- Model training, containerization, deployment, and performance monitoring

- Xsolis is an AI-driven technology company with a human-centered approach, fostering collaboration between healthcare providers and payers through real-time transparency, objective data for increased accuracy and alignment of medical necessity decisions, and more efficient outcomes. Dragonfly®, its AI-driven proprietary platform, is the first and only solution to use real-time predictive analytics to continuously assign an objective medical necessity score and assess the anticipated level of care for every patient, enabling more efficiency across the healthcare system. Xsolis is headquartered in Franklin, Tennessee.

results.title: Applied AI Engineer, Clinical Informatics job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3051041
highlights:
Applied AI Engineer, Clinical Informatics job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3051041
[...]
# Applied AI Engineer, Clinical Informatics
[...]
Employer Eli Lilly and Company Location Boston, Massachusetts Start date May 23, 2026
[...]
We are seeking a highly specialized Applied AI Engineer Clinical Informatician to lead research at the intersection of completed clinical trial datasets and biobank-linked population data. This is fundamentally a hands-on research role (not operational trial management), where you will be an individual contributor. Your core mission is to build the systems and tools that extract, define, and contextualize patient phenotypes from locked trial databases, real-world data, and biobank cohorts, that will turn archived data that can generate translational insight that shapes the next generation of clinical research.
[...]
You will work with rich, already-collected datasets: locked trial databases, archived omics profiles, longitudinal electronic health records, and population-scale biobank cohorts. Your mandate is to build the AI and ML systems that make these datasets manageable and ready for detailed analysis. This role suits someone who thinks like a scientist, builds like an engineer, and communicates like a clinician.
[...]
Key Responsibilities
[...]
AI & Machine Learning for Translational Discovery
[...]
- Develop and deploy agentic AI applications that enable natural language interaction with clinical data
[...]
- Ground AI outputs in validated biological knowledge, for example implementing RAG pipelines anchored in biomedical ontologies (HPO, Gene Ontology, MeSH, DrugBank), clinical trial registries, and curated pathway databases
[...]
- Deploy unsupervised and self-supervised learning approaches like clustering, representation learning, contrastive learning to discover latent patient archetypes and molecular disease subtypes across trial and biobank data
[...]
- Deploy survival models and dynamic treatment regime estimators using combined clinical and omics features
[...]
- AI tooling to harmonize heterogeneous trial and biobank datasets to common data representations
[...]
- Evaluate and monitor model performance, safety, and reliability in production environments
[...]
- Building pipelines for locked clinical trial databases (SDTM, ADaM) to conduct secondary and exploratory research beyond primary endpoints
[...]
- Deploy ML workflows to identify trial subgroup effects, treatment heterogeneity, and responder/non-responder signatures from completed trial data
[...]
- Mine adverse event narratives, clinical notes, and investigator comments using NLP to surface latent safety signals not captured in structured endpoints in biobanks and clinical datasets
[...]
- M.S. in Biomedical Informatics, Computational Biology, Bioinformatics, Statistical Genetics , Epidemiology, or a closely related quantitative field or an MD/PhD with equivalent depth in translational data science with 6+ years of research experience working with clinical trial datasets (SDTM/ADaM), biobank data, or large-scale population health data in an academic, pharmaceutical, or research institute setting
[...]
- Demonstrated use of AI tools in production environments for clinical data analysis
[...]
- Expert proficiency in Python and/or R for statistical modelling and ML; strong command of SQL and experience with cloud-based research computing environments (ideally DNAnexus, AWS, GCP, Azure, or HPC clusters)
[...]
- Familiar with advanced generative AI methods like finetuning of LLMs. Building and training foundation models from scratch. High performance computing environments
[...]
- Demonstrated experience applying ML methods including survival analysis, causal inference, NLP, and deep learning to clinical or genomic research questions
[...]
- Track record of peer-reviewed publications in clinical AI,
[...]
- Experience with knowledge graph construction, graph ML, or ontology-driven reasoning for biomedical discovery

results.title: Empathy in Healthcare Through Responsible AI | Elevance Health
highlights:
# Empathy in Healthcare Through Responsible AI | Elevance Health
[...]
Empathy in Healthcare Through Responsible AI | Elevance Health Skip to main
[...]
# How AI Creates More Space for Empathy in Healthcare
[...]
http://www.elevancehealth.com/our-approach-to-health/consumer-centered-health-system/how-ai-creates-more-space-for-empathy-in-healthcare
[...]
- Artificial intelligence creates room for empathy in healthcare by reducing administrative tasks and helping associates more quickly understand members’ needs.
- Tools like AI-powered transcription and text analysis bring a new level of understanding to phone calls and other conversations.
- Responsible AI principles ensure these innovations are used ethically, securely, transparently, and in ways that strengthen human-centered care.
[...]
Artificial intelligence encourages greater efficiency, but in healthcare, one of its more powerful contributions may be far more human: creating room for empathy. Rather than replacing the human touch in the customer experience, at Elevance Health, AI strengthens it. By helping service teams more easily access information, AI also gives them the time to better relate to the health situations of the people they serve.
[...]
With the help of AI, service associates no longer have to start from scratch when a member reaches out. By stitching together critical details about a member’s whole health and past healthcare experiences — within established legal and consumer protections governing the use of such information — AI can help create a more complete picture of that member. This makes it easier for a service associate to help them.
[...]
With tools like transcription and text analysis, AI also brings a new level of understanding to phone calls and other conversations; the text can be instantly analyzed to help identify how members may have felt during and after their interactions with us. Service representatives can reach out when needed or appropriate, extending empathy beyond just the initial call, text, or email.
[...]
Healthcare has traditionally demanded that service teams navigate multiple systems, take notes, and search for information, requiring them to juggle a heavy administrative load while attempting to listen intently to a member they’re trying to help. AI can more quickly find relevant information and manage routine processes in the background, freeing up time for a more meaningful person-to-person connection. With fewer manual tasks, service associates can listen carefully, ask thoughtful questions, and offer reassurance.
[...]
## Balancing AI Innovation with Accountability and Responsibility
[...]
While AI can foster empathy, its use must be grounded in responsibility. Data must be used ethically, transparently, and securely, or trust will erode.
[...]
Elevance Health’s Responsible AI (RAI) approach to technology design, deployment, and ongoing use ensures that regulations and laws — as well as our values, ethics, and principles — stand at the center of creation and decision-making around artificial intelligence. Every AI product or service is designed, developed, deployed, and used in a way that is consistent with our AI guiding principles: Fair, Private and Secure, Robust, Accountable, and Explainable and Transparent.
[...]
AI can more quickly find relevant information and manage routine processes in the background, freeing up time for a more meaningful person-to-person connection. With fewer manual tasks, service associates can listen carefully, ask thoughtful questions, and offer reassurance.
[...]
These principles set a foundation that allows AI to move beyond simple efficiency to support a more human-centered service experience — one marked by active understanding, holistic support, and treating every person with the empathy they deserve.
[...]
Ratnakar Lavu is Elevance Health’s Chief Digital Information Officer, and Saurabh Tandon is Vice President and Chief Experience Officer.

The Applied Intelligence for Discovery (AI4D) team is a newly formed group within Lilly Research Laboratories that operates at the intersection of scientific delivery and core platform development. AI4D’s mission is to connecting scientists to petabyte-scale data through natural language interfaces, automated analysis workflows, and intelligent search — and to convert early deployments into repeatable system standards and evaluation practices that scale across therapeutic areas.

As a Generative AI Engineer, you will design, build, and operate the core AI systems that power this transformation: retrieval-augmented generation over internal scientific documents, text-to-SQL over complex omics databases, agentic workflows that automate multi-step analyses, and the evaluation infrastructure that able the next-generation of medicines for patients.

Design agentic AI workflows that chain database queries, bioinformatics tools, literature search, and visualization into automated multi-step scientific analyses

Evaluate and integrate emerging orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, custom architectures) for scientific use cases

Build evaluation frameworks measuring accuracy, reliability, and scientific validity of AI outputs

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group, we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will contribute to our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at relevant drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Design, implement, and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, writing robust, performance-critical code and contributing to core infrastructure.
[...]
Model Improvement & Reasoning: Develop and execute strategies to systematically improve performance on scientific tasks, including long-horizon task completion and complex reasoning challenges.
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into concrete machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria.
[...]
Evaluation & Benchmarks: Design and implement evaluation methodologies to assess model capabilities relevant to biological research, working with domain experts to establish benchmarks and curate high-quality data.
[...]
Research-to-Production: Collaborate closely with researchers to translate ideas and prototypes into scalable, production-ready systems.
[...]
As a Machine Learning Scientist:
[...]
Focus: You focus on the execution of defined projects. You are responsible for writing clean, efficient code to test specific hypotheses regarding reasoning and alignment.
[...]
Engineering: You contribute to the maintenance of the training infrastructure and data pipelines, ensuring experiments run reliably on our clusters.
[...]
Collaboration: You work closely with senior scientists to implement novel algorithms, translating research papers into working prototypes.
[...]
BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, or a related quantitative field with 2+ years of relevant work experience. Or Ph. D. with 0-2 years relevant work experience.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including post-training techniques to enhance domain knowledge, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Engineering: Strong software engineering skills and experience working with high-performance computing systems.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data).
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]
The expected salary
[...]
New York City
[...]
– 274,00
[...]
. Actual pay will be
[...]
. A discretionary annual bonus may be available based on individual and Company performance. This position also qualifies for the benefits detailed at the link provided

Genentech is a biotechnology research company that specializes in genetic testing and personalized medicines.
